title: WRONGFUL CONVICTION SUPPORT
description: IRN documents cases of prosecutorial misconduct and wrongful conviction across Hampton Roads. Through the CJRRP, families receive a case manager, an attorney referral, and a public solidarity statement within 72 hours.

title: LEGAL EDUCATION & KNOW YOUR RIGHTS
description: We deliver Know Your Rights workshops in Hampton Roads neighborhoods — covering police encounters, pretrial detention, school discipline, and tenant rights — at an 8th-grade reading level with zero legal jargon.

title: FINES, COSTS & GARNISHMENT RELIEF
description: Court fines and cost garnishment trap people in cycles of poverty and re-incarceration. IRN identifies relief pathways, connects individuals to legal aid, and documents patterns of predatory fee assessment for legislative advocacy.

title: COMMUNITY SERVICE PROGRAM
description: Alternative accountability rooted in community, not punishment. IRN supports restorative approaches that address harm without feeding the carceral system.

title: VICTIM / WITNESS ADVOCACY
description: Survivors and witnesses navigate complex legal systems alone. IRN provides trauma-informed accompaniment, documentation support, and rights education at every stage of the legal process.

title: CRIME PREVENTION THROUGH INVESTMENT
description: Safety comes from investment, not surveillance. IRN advocates for the community programs, mental health infrastructure, and economic resources that actually reduce harm.

title: COMMUNITY HARM REPORTING (CHRT)
description: IRN's privacy-first reporting tool documents civil rights incidents anonymously. Zero-knowledge architecture means IRN cannot be compelled to identify reporters. Your account. Your data. Your choice.
